sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) the skin of a man (so caned perhaps from nakedness, see the root No. II), Exo 34:30, 3.5; Levit. 13:2 Job 7:5, and so frequently. עֹור שִׁנַּיִם skin of the teeth, i.e. the gums, Job 19:20 (on this passage see under the root מָלַט ). As to the words Job 19:20, see under the root נָקַף . Poet. used of the body, the life, Job 2:4, עֹור בְּעַד עֹור “skin for skin;” i.e. life for life. Job 18:13, “parts of his skin;” i.e. the members of his body.

(2) the hide of animals, Gen 3:21 pl. עֹורֹות Gen 27:16 also used of hides artificially prepared, leather, Lev 4:11 13:48 .
